title: Third Time, First Trophy
title_candidates: Bridesmaid No More: Alex Baker on the Algarve
From Champion to Spoon: Patterson's Algarve Reversal
theme: Inversion in the Algarve sun: the two-time bridesmaid finally lifts the Trophy while the defending champion collects the Spoon — and David Mullin, almost incidentally, rewrites the Boavista record book.
opening_hook: Open on the symmetry of the final scoreboard — Alex Baker, runner-up in TEGs 8 and 9, finally a champion; John Patterson, the man whose Trophy he inherits, holding the Spoon. Then rewind to how the inversion was built.
foreshadow: Alex Baker arrives as a two-time runner-up — bridesmaid in TEGs 8 and 9 — chasing a first Trophy.
John Patterson arrives as defending Trophy champion (TEG 9), with no Spoon on his CV.
David Mullin lands in the Algarve on a run of four straight Jackets, including back-to-back going in, and Boavista is effectively his home course (10 prior visits).
Stuart Neumann turns up carrying back-to-back Spoons and looking like the obvious candidate for a third.

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Mullin's record-breaking R1 49 opened an 8-point cushion, but Alex Baker stalked him with 41 and 45, took outright lead on R3 H6 (b31), survived a brief Williams takeover on H13 (b36), then closed R3 leading by 11 — a margin he matched exactly in R4 with the 51-point round that broke the all-time TEG record (b04).

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Mullin's +4 R1 at Boavista (b03) — the best gross round in TEG history and a new Boavista course record by seven shots (cr01) — opened a 14-shot Jacket lead before lunch on day one. He led wire-to-wire, never gave up the front, and finished +51 to win by 12 (b02).

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Defending Trophy champion to last place in one TEG: Patterson dropped to bottom on R1 H4 and never climbed off. The damage was four sextuple/quintuple bogeys (b15, b16, b26, b27), a 19-shot bleed across R3 holes 11-15 (b38), and a closing 21-point round — his worst ever (b34). Final total 112 (b30).

headline_candidates: The Record Falls on Day One
rounds.angle: Mullin's 75 gross — a Boavista course record by seven shots, the best gross round in TEG history, and a 49-point haul second only to what was still to come — opens 8- and 14-shot leads in Trophy and Jacket; Patterson sinks to last on H4 and stays there.

headline_candidates: Three Personal Bests and a Ten at the First
rounds.chosen_headline: Three Personal Bests and a Ten at the First
rounds.angle: Palmares Praia/Alvor — new ground for everyone — yields personal-best Stableford rounds for Alex Baker, Williams and (gross) Jon Baker's +10, the third-best gross round in TEG history; Patterson opens with a sextuple-bogey 10 at the 1st and Neumann posts another 10 at the 18th.

headline_candidates: The Lead Changes Hands at Alvor
rounds.angle: On R3 H6 Alex Baker takes outright Trophy lead for the first time (b31); Williams snatches it back on H13 (b36) but Baker re-takes it on H15 and pushes the cushion to 11 by the close. Patterson's tournament dies in a 19-shot stretch across 11-15.

Back at Boavista, Alex Baker posts 51 points — the best round in TEG history (b04) — built on a 21-point burst across holes 5-10 (b06); Patterson signs off with his worst-ever 21; Mullin pockets the Jacket; Williams sets a personal-best gross total.

players.arc: Two-time runner-up turned champion: opens with 41, climbs to outright lead on R3 H6, and closes with the highest-scoring round in TEG history. A first Trophy, a personal-best gross total, three personal-best Stableford rounds in succession.
Almost a sideshow to his own dominance: an 8th Jacket, a new Boavista course record (75), the best gross round in TEG history (+4), and yet still beaten by 11 on the Trophy metric.
Personal-best Stableford and gross rounds in R2, a brief outright Trophy lead on R3 H13, a new personal-best gross total (+76), and Trophy runner-up — the closest he has come without winning.
The habitual Jacket bridesmaid — runner-up in three of the last four — finishes second again, but with a personal-best gross total (+63) and the third-best gross round in TEG history (R2 +10).
From defending Trophy champion (TEG 9) to Wooden Spoon in twelve months: bottom from R1 H4, four blow-ups of quintuple bogey or worse, and a closing 21-point round that is his worst on record.

Arrived holding back-to-back Spoons and looking the favourite for a third; escapes only because Patterson collapses harder. Still posts three quintuple-or-worse blow-ups, including consecutive 10s at the 18th in R1 and R2.

venue_notes: TEG's 4th visit to the Algarve. Bookended at Boavista (the 11th and 12th TEG rounds there, and effectively a home course for the long-servers — Mullin, Jon Baker, Williams and Neumann all on their 11th and 12th visits), with two days at the new Palmares 27-holer in the middle (Praia/Alvor R2, Alvor/Lagos R3 — both fresh ground). Boavista is the canvas for the record-breaking: Mullin's 75 (a new course record by seven across 50 prior visits) and Alex Baker's 51-point closer. Personal bests at Boavista for Mullin, Williams and Jon Baker all land here.
decisive_moments: Trophy: R3 H6 — Alex Baker takes outright Trophy lead (b31) and is never headed across the next 30 holes.
Green Jacket: R1 H6 — Mullin moves clear at Boavista (cr01) and opens a 14-shot Jacket lead before the round is out.
Wooden Spoon: R1 H4 — Patterson drops to last place (decisive_drop) and stays there for every subsequent hole of the TEG.

payoffs.seed: Alex Baker's two-time bridesmaid history (TEGs 8 & 9 runner-up).
payoffs.resolves_in: How the Trophy was won + Round 4
payoffs.payoff: Wins the Trophy by 11 on the back of three consecutive personal-best Stableford rounds and the highest-scoring round in TEG history (b04).

payoffs.seed: Patterson the defending Trophy champion, no Spoon on the CV.
payoffs.payoff: Drops to last on R1 H4 and stays there all week; collects the Spoon on 112 (b30) twelve months after lifting the Trophy.

payoffs.seed: Mullin's four-Jacket streak and Boavista home-course familiarity.
payoffs.resolves_in: Round 1 + How the Jacket was won
payoffs.payoff: R1 75 gross — new Boavista course record by seven shots (cr01), best gross round in TEG history (b03), 14-shot Jacket lead inside one round, an 8th Jacket secured (b02).
Escapes the Spoon only because Patterson collapses harder; still posts consecutive 10s at the 18th in R1 and R2 (b17, b18) and a 13-shot bleed across R2 holes 13-15 (b77).
